mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which translates into robust consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:strong employment typically correlates with economic growth
regime_hint: any regime with positive employment growth
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the federal funds rate signals an aggressive monetary tightening cycle by the central bank, which raises borrowing costs, dampens economic activity, and reduces corporate valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by 0.5 percentage points or more within 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:central bank rate hikes typically precede market slowdowns
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS increases by 0.5pp or more over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of robust retail sales growth and a declining unemployment rate indicates strong consumer confidence and purchasing power, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND UNRATE 3-month change < -0.1%
failure_condition: Periods where RSAFS growth is strong and UNRATE is declining do not show statistically significant positive SPX 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts during an early-cycle recovery indicate renewed investment and consumer confidence in the real estate sector, which has significant spillover effects on other industries, driving overall economic growth and positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts are a leading indicator of economic activity
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of instances where HOUST shows strong growth during an early-cycle recovery.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects healthy manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, signaling robust economic activity that supports corporate revenues and short-term equity market gains over a weekly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production is a key measure of economic health
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO month-over-month growth over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending, lower corporate profits, and ultimately negative S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.4 percentage points or more over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:rising unemployment is a hallmark of recessions
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by 0.4pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and accelerating while the Federal Reserve is simultaneously raising interest rates (FEDFUNDS), it creates a challenging environment for equities due to higher discount rates and eroding purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:high inflation and rising rates are detrimental to equity valuations
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are consistently positive or flat during periods where both CPIAUCSL growth is high and FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Robust and accelerating GDP growth indicates broad-based economic health and strong aggregate demand, which directly translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and sustained positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is the primary measure of economic expansion
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of instances where GDPC1 shows strong growth above 3.0%.
